description.value:
A deep red gemstone, cleanly cut, with an internal brightness that catches every nearby light source. Any appraiser would value it at 150 gp immediately. A more patient appraiser, given time, might wonder why the brightness moves.

Curse. The gem is worthless - an alchemical simulacrum. Any transaction using it as payment fails within 24 hours when the buyer discovers the truth. More critically, a character carrying it has disadvantage on Deception checks, as the gem radiates a subtle magical tell that experienced traders recognise instinctively.

unidentified.description: A deep red gemstone, beautifully cut. The appraiser thinks it's worth about 150 gp. They seem confident.

cursedMeta.decoyAppearance: A brilliant cut red garnet. Estimated value 150 gp.
cursedMeta.trueNature: Worthless alchemical fake. Carrier has disadvantage on Deception checks.

curse.name: The Fool's Gleam
curse.description: The gem is a worthless simulacrum. A character carrying it has disadvantage on Deception checks as experienced eyes catch a subtle magical tell.

hints: Appraisers agree too quickly; the inner fire shifts when nobody is looking.

escalation.effect: Small contradictions in how light catches the facets during haggling.
